items.headline: Auto Sales Use Proposed at El Camino Real Commercial Site
items.summary: The Planning Commission is scheduled to review a special development permit for 975 W. El Camino Real to allow auto sales on the existing commercial property. The project involves site improvements like repaving and landscaping but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: City launches financial assistance program for water customers
items.summary: San José approved a pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers, addressing affordability concerns for residents during a period of rising utility costs.

items.headline: City in closed session over Cuesta Park water main incident litigation
items.summary: Mountain View is addressing potential litigation related to a water main incident at Cuesta Park and negotiating real estate matters involving two properties on Clyde Avenue. The closed session discussion involves legal counsel and real property negotiators but specific details of the incident are not yet public.

items.headline: Planning Commission to consider 27-unit townhome project with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled review of a residential development featuring 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. The project represents new housing in Cupertino, with a mix of market-rate and below-market-rate homes.

items.headline: City Council to hear 100% affordable housing project at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The Council will hold a public hearing on a six-story, 72-unit affordable housing development proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue on a city-owned parking lot. The project requests density bonus concessions under state law to make the development feasible on the 0.43-acre site.

items.headline: Palo Alto building receives permit for major electrical infrastructure upgrade
items.summary: A significant electrical infrastructure project has been permitted for 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development operations. The work includes installation of electrical transformers, switches, and conduit extending to a substation on Hanover Drive, signaling expanded R&D activity at the commercial site.

items.headline: City Council adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: Campbell City Council adopted a resolution on May 5 prohibiting the use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy limits access to city facilities and resources for certain federal operations.

items.headline: Council declares hazardous vegetation a public nuisance
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted a resolution formally declaring hazardous brush and vegetation a public nuisance, enabling the city to order property owners to remove fire hazards. This gives the city enforcement authority to reduce wildfire risk on private land.
